title: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Downloaded OOP JSON exams imported from Downloads.

exams.title: Lecture 1 - Review
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Pointers, arrays, functions, recursion, structs, and arrays of structs.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: The array name points to the first element, so p = a makes *p equal to a[0].

questions.prompt: After p = a; p++; which array element does *p refer to?
questions.explanation: Pointer arithmetic moves by one element of the pointer type, not by one byte in source-level thinking.

questions.prompt: Which statements are true about passing an array to a function as int* p?
choices: The function can read array elements through *p.
The function automatically knows the array length.
p++ can move to the next element.
Changing *p can change the caller's array element.
questions.explanation: A pointer gives access to elements, but array length is not carried with the pointer.

questions.prompt: In C++, a struct can group different data fields under one name.
questions.explanation: A struct is a user-defined aggregate, useful before classes for grouping related data.

questions.prompt: Which loop correctly prints exactly 3 elements using pointer p?
questions.explanation: For 3 elements, valid iteration counts are i = 0, 1, 2, so i < 3 is correct.

questions.prompt: What is the dangerous part of recursion?
choices: It may never stop without a base case.
questions.explanation: A recursive function needs a stopping condition; otherwise the calls continue until stack failure.

exams.title: Lecture 2 - Programming Paradigms
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Imperative, procedural, functional, declarative, and object-oriented thinking.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best describes imperative programming?
choices: Describe what result is needed, not how.
questions.explanation: Imperative code focuses on how the computer should do the task step by step.

questions.prompt: Procedural programming mainly adds what idea to imperative programming?
questions.explanation: Procedural programming subdivides a program into functions or procedures.

questions.prompt: Which ideas match OOP as presented in the lecture?
choices: Each object groups information and actions.
questions.explanation: OOP moves from one long procedure toward entities that own data and behavior.

questions.prompt: A programming paradigm is the same thing as a programming language.
questions.explanation: A paradigm is a style or set of principles. A language may support one or many paradigms.

questions.prompt: In declarative programming, the programmer usually emphasizes:
choices: Every loop counter and update step
questions.explanation: Declarative style hides many mechanics and focuses on the desired result.

questions.prompt: Which comparison is most accurate?
choices: Structured code has no functions; OOP has functions.
OOP combines related data and functions into objects.
OOP removes the need for data.
Procedural and OOP are identical names.
questions.explanation: The important shift is packaging state and behavior together.

exams.title: Lecture 3 - Classes
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Class syntax, objects, access, setters/getters, constructors, destructors, and scope resolution.

questions.prompt: In a C++ class, members are private by default unless another access label is written. What does this mean?
choices: They can be accessed using any object.
Only member/friend code can access them directly.
They are copied into every function.
questions.explanation: Class default access is private, so outside code cannot directly touch those data members.

questions.prompt: Why should st.setdata(1, "Osman") be preferred over st.stno = 1 when stno is private?
choices: It allows validation/filtering before storing data.
questions.explanation: Setters protect the object by controlling how data enters it.

questions.prompt: Which function name is a constructor for class elevator?
questions.explanation: A constructor has the same name as the class and has no return type.

questions.prompt: A destructor can receive parameters so it can destroy the object in different ways.
questions.explanation: A destructor has no parameters and no return type.

questions.prompt: What does elevator::display() mean?
choices: display is defined outside the class but belongs to elevator.
questions.explanation: The scope resolution operator connects the outside definition to the class.

questions.prompt: Which constructor is called?
questions.explanation: Passing 11 matches the constructor that accepts one integer.

exams.title: Lecture 4 - Objects, Static Data, and Overloading
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Object memory, static class data, object arguments, returning objects, function and constructor overloading.

questions.prompt: If a class has 3 objects, how many copies of non-static data members exist?
choices: Zero until a function is called
questions.explanation: Each object owns its own ordinary data members.

questions.prompt: If a class has 3 objects, how many copies of a static data member exist?
questions.explanation: Static class data is shared among all objects of the class.

questions.prompt: Which changes can make function overloading valid?
choices: Different parameter order when types differ
questions.explanation: Return type alone is not enough for overload resolution.

questions.prompt: If no constructor is defined in a class, what can the compiler provide?
questions.explanation: The compiler can generate a default constructor when no constructor is user-declared.

questions.prompt: What value is most likely printed by c2.get_count() after these operations?
choices: Undefined because c2 shares c1 data
questions.explanation: c2 is incremented twice; ordinary data is separate for each object.

questions.prompt: When a member function returns an object, what does it allow?
choices: Assigning a computed object result, such as dist3 = dist1.add_dist(dist2)
questions.explanation: Returning objects makes object computations composable.

exams.title: Lecture 5 - Unary Operator Overloading
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Unary operators, prefix ++, return values, temporary objects, nameless objects, and this.

questions.prompt: Which declaration overloads prefix ++ as a member function for class Counter?
questions.explanation: The keyword operator is followed by the operator symbol.

questions.prompt: Why does c1 = ++c2 fail if operator++ returns void?
choices: Assignment needs a Counter value to assign.
questions.explanation: The expression ++c2 must produce something assignable to c1.

questions.prompt: Which are valid strategies mentioned for returning from overloaded unary ++?
questions.explanation: The lecture mentions temporary object, nameless temporary object, and this.

questions.prompt: Why does return Counter(count); require a constructor with one argument?
choices: It creates a nameless temporary object initialized by count.
questions.explanation: Counter(count) constructs a temporary Counter object using the one-argument constructor.

questions.prompt: Overloading ++ for Counter changes how ++ works for int variables.
questions.explanation: Built-in types still use built-in operator behavior.

questions.prompt: Which declaration is the usual member form for postfix ++?
questions.explanation: The dummy int parameter distinguishes postfix from prefix.

exams.title: Lecture 6 - Binary Operator Overloading
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Binary operators, operator+, member operand roles, and object/int limitations.

questions.prompt: In Sedative = Panadol + Aspirin; with member operator+, which object calls the operator?
questions.explanation: For member binary operators, the left operand is the calling object.

questions.prompt: In Items operator+(Items A), what is A usually?
questions.explanation: The right operand is passed as the parameter.

questions.prompt: If Panadol has 5 and Aspirin has 6, what should Sedative store after Panadol + Aspirin?
questions.explanation: operator+ should add the item_count values and return the result object.
questions.code:
Items Panadol(5), Aspirin(6), Sedative;
Sedative = Panadol + Aspirin;

questions.prompt: Which operators cannot be overloaded in standard C++?
questions.explanation: Scope resolution, member access, and conditional cannot be overloaded. + can be overloaded.

questions.prompt: Why can Panadol + 7 work while 15 + Panadol may fail with only a member operator?
choices: The left operand must be the object that owns the member operator.
7 is always converted to Panadol automatically both ways.
questions.explanation: A member operator is found from the left operand's class.

questions.prompt: Which return style creates a nameless result object?
questions.explanation: Items(count) constructs and returns an unnamed temporary object.

exams.title: Lecture 7 - Inheritance Concepts
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Inheritance definition, parent/child classes, and forms of inheritance.

questions.prompt: Inheritance means:
choices: Packing data and functions into one component
Acquiring properties of a parent/base class in a child/derived class
Creating many functions with one name
Hiding all code from the compiler
questions.explanation: Inheritance lets a derived class reuse and extend a base class.

questions.prompt: Vehicle -> Automobile -> FamilyCar is best described as:
questions.explanation: A chain across more than one level is multilevel inheritance.

questions.prompt: One base class inherited by many derived classes is:
questions.explanation: Hierarchical inheritance has one parent with multiple children.

questions.prompt: One derived class inheriting from more than one base class is:
questions.explanation: Multiple inheritance combines more than one base class.

questions.prompt: Which are listed inheritance types in the lecture?
questions.explanation: The lecture lists single, multilevel, multiple, hierarchical, and hybrid.

questions.prompt: Inheritance and encapsulation are the same concept because both use classes.
questions.explanation: Encapsulation packages/hides data; inheritance reuses/extends parent class features.

exams.title: Lecture 8 - Inheritance Syntax and Modes
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Base/derived syntax, public/protected/private inheritance, and multiple inheritance examples.

questions.prompt: Which syntax correctly declares Student as publicly derived from Person?
questions.explanation: C++ uses a colon followed by the inheritance access specifier and base class.

questions.prompt: Under public inheritance, a public base member becomes what in the derived class?
questions.explanation: Public inheritance preserves public as public.

questions.prompt: Under protected inheritance, a public base member becomes what in the derived class?
questions.explanation: Protected inheritance converts inherited public and protected members to protected.

questions.prompt: Under private inheritance, protected base members become:
questions.explanation: Private inheritance makes inherited public/protected members private in the derived class.

questions.prompt: A derived class can directly access private members of the base class.
questions.explanation: Private base members remain inaccessible directly to derived classes.

questions.prompt: class Bat : public Mammal, public Winged_Animal is an example of:
questions.explanation: Bat has two base classes.

exams.title: Lecture 9 - Polymorphism and Virtual Base Classes
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Virtual functions, runtime polymorphism, overriding, and virtual inheritance.

questions.prompt: Runtime polymorphism in C++ is mainly achieved using:
questions.explanation: Virtual dispatch chooses the derived override at runtime through a base pointer/reference.

questions.prompt: A virtual function should have the same prototype in the base and derived class when overriding.
questions.explanation: Different signatures may hide or overload instead of overriding.

questions.prompt: Why can shape* ptrarr[100] call different draw() implementations?
choices: Each pointer may point to a different derived shape object.
questions.explanation: A base pointer plus virtual draw() dispatches based on the actual object.

questions.prompt: Which statements about virtual functions are true?
choices: They are declared with virtual in the base class.
questions.explanation: Constructors cannot be virtual; virtual destructors are possible and often important.

questions.prompt: What problem does virtual base class inheritance solve?
choices: Duplicate base subobjects in diamond inheritance
questions.explanation: Virtual base inheritance shares one common base subobject.

questions.prompt: Without virtual inheritance, why is basedata ambiguous in Grandchild?
choices: Grandchild has two Parent subobjects through Child1 and Child2.
questions.explanation: The compiler cannot know which inherited Parent copy is meant.

exams.title: Lecture 10 - Friend Functions and Classes
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Friend functions, bridge functions, friend classes, access permissions, and one-way friendship.

questions.prompt: A friend function is best described as:
choices: A member function that is always public
A non-member function allowed to access private/protected class data
questions.explanation: A friend is not a class member, but receives special access.

questions.prompt: The keyword friend is written in the function definition outside the class.
questions.explanation: friend appears in the declaration inside the class, not in the outside definition.

questions.prompt: Why are friends described as bridges?
choices: They can operate on private data from two different classes.
They make every class inherit every other class.
They remove the need for objects.
questions.explanation: A friend function can be declared by two classes and access both private parts.

questions.prompt: Which are disadvantages or risks of friend functions?
choices: They access private members from outside normal member functions.
questions.explanation: Friend functions are powerful but should be used carefully.

questions.prompt: If ClassB is declared friend inside ClassA, what follows?
choices: ClassA automatically becomes friend of ClassB.
ClassB can access ClassA private/protected members.
Friendship is inherited by all child classes automatically.
questions.explanation: Friendship is granted one way; it is not automatically mutual.

questions.prompt: Which call style fits a friend function int sum(point p)?
questions.explanation: A friend function is not called with the object's dot operator.

exams.title: Lecture 11 - Templates
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Function templates, class templates, multiple template parameters, generic swap, and typename/class.

questions.prompt: The main purpose of templates is to:
questions.explanation: Templates let one function or class work with different data types.

questions.prompt: Which function template header is valid?
questions.explanation: C++ template declarations commonly use template<class T> or template<typename T>.

questions.prompt: Why can sum(int, double) be difficult for template<class T> T sum(T, T)?
choices: Both parameters must deduce the same T.
int cannot be a template type.
questions.explanation: A single T parameter expects both arguments to match the same deduced type.

questions.prompt: Which can solve mixed-type template parameters?
questions.explanation: Multiple template parameters and explicit arguments help with mixed types.

questions.prompt: In many template declarations, class and typename are semantically equivalent.
questions.explanation: For type parameters, template<class T> and template<typename T> are generally interchangeable.

questions.prompt: What does func_swap(num1, num2) do here?
choices: Compilation must fail because T is int
questions.explanation: The template swaps by reference, so the caller's variables exchange values.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 1 - Foundations to Classes
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Mixed tricky questions from review, paradigms, classes, constructors, static data, and overloading.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: p starts at a[0], then p += 2 points at a[2], whose value is 8.

questions.prompt: Which statement best separates encapsulation from inheritance?
choices: Encapsulation is only about ++; inheritance is only about templates.
Inheritance hides all private data automatically from the base itself.
questions.explanation: Encapsulation protects object internals; inheritance creates parent-child relationships.

questions.prompt: Which situations require overloaded functions rather than one identical signature?
choices: Different type order with same count
questions.explanation: Return type alone cannot distinguish overloads.

questions.prompt: Which constructor is called by Student st;
questions.explanation: No arguments means a default constructor is needed.

questions.prompt: All objects of a class share ordinary non-static data members.
questions.explanation: Ordinary data is per object; static data is shared.

questions.prompt: Which is the best reason to keep data private?
choices: To force controlled access through member functions
questions.explanation: Private data supports controlled changes and safer object states.

questions.prompt: Which paradigm says 'focus on what result is needed' most strongly?
questions.explanation: Declarative code hides the step-by-step mechanics.

questions.prompt: What happens conceptually?
choices: A new object is created only if count was static
questions.explanation: Prefix ++ calls the overloaded member function and increments count.
questions.code:
Counter c;
++c;

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 2 - Operators and Inheritance
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Unary/binary operators, inheritance forms, access modes, and object behavior.

questions.prompt: In a member overload for d1 + d2, why is d2 usually a parameter?
choices: Because d1 is the implicit calling object.
questions.explanation: Member binary operators use the left operand as this.

questions.prompt: Select the true access transformations.
choices: Public inheritance keeps public as public.
Protected inheritance makes public base members protected.
Private inheritance makes protected base members private.
Public inheritance makes private base members public.
questions.explanation: Private members remain inaccessible directly; they do not become public.

questions.prompt: Which inheritance type combines more than one form, such as multiple plus multilevel?
questions.explanation: Hybrid inheritance is a combination of inheritance types.

questions.prompt: operator++(int) is commonly used to distinguish postfix ++ from prefix ++.
questions.explanation: The int is a dummy parameter used for postfix overload resolution.

questions.prompt: Which operator cannot be overloaded?
questions.explanation: The scope resolution operator cannot be overloaded.

questions.prompt: What is the correct interpretation?
choices: Aspirin is the caller and Panadol is the parameter.
Panadol is the caller and Aspirin is the parameter.
questions.explanation: The left operand calls the member operator+.
questions.code: Sedative = Panadol + Aspirin;

questions.prompt: A derived class cannot directly access which base members?
questions.explanation: Private base members are not directly accessible in the derived class.

questions.prompt: Vehicle -> Bus and Vehicle -> SportCar is:
questions.explanation: One base class with multiple derived classes is hierarchical.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 3 - Polymorphism, Friends, Templates
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Virtual dispatch, virtual base classes, friend access, and generic programming.

questions.prompt: Which feature makes ptr->draw() call Ball::draw() when ptr points to a Ball object?
choices: virtual draw() in the base class
questions.explanation: Virtual dispatch uses the actual object type at runtime.

questions.prompt: Friendship in C++ is automatically mutual.
questions.explanation: If B is friend of A, A is not automatically friend of B.

questions.prompt: Which are valid reasons to use templates?
choices: Avoid duplicate code for many data types
Force all parameters to be int
questions.explanation: Templates are about generic, reusable type-independent code.

questions.prompt: What does virtual public Parent do in diamond inheritance?
questions.explanation: Virtual inheritance prevents duplicate Parent subobjects.

questions.prompt: Which friend function declaration inside class point is plausible?
questions.explanation: The friend keyword precedes the function declaration inside the class.

questions.prompt: Why can template<class T> void swap(T&, T&) change caller variables?
questions.explanation: References allow the function to modify the original variables.

questions.prompt: Virtual functions are mainly used for runtime polymorphism.
questions.explanation: The function call is resolved at runtime based on the actual object.

questions.prompt: Which is the most accurate risk of friend functions?
choices: They can violate data hiding if overused.
They are required for every class.
questions.explanation: Friend access should be limited because it bypasses normal encapsulation.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 4 - Code Tracing and Corrections
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - Closed-choice tracing and fix-selection across all lecture topics.

questions.prompt: What does this print?
questions.explanation: func_swap uses references, so x and y are exchanged.

questions.prompt: Which fix allows c1 = ++c2 for class Counter?
choices: Make operator++ return Counter or Counter&.
questions.explanation: The expression must produce a Counter-compatible value.

questions.prompt: Which access mode should model an is-a relationship most naturally?
questions.explanation: Public inheritance preserves the substitutable is-a relation.

questions.prompt: Which are closed-choice exam-safe question types?
questions.explanation: This app avoids open-ended typed answers.

questions.prompt: A class template object can be created as myclass<int> obj(1, 2);
questions.explanation: Template type arguments are provided when creating the object.

questions.prompt: What does *p mean after int* p = &x;?
questions.explanation: Dereferencing p accesses the value at the address p stores.

questions.prompt: What is printed if print() is not virtual and bp is Base*?
questions.explanation: Without virtual dispatch, the base pointer type controls the call.
questions.code:
Base* bp = new Derived;
bp->print();

questions.prompt: Which is the best fix for diamond ambiguity of basedata?
choices: Use virtual inheritance for the shared Parent base.
questions.explanation: Virtual base classes make Child1 and Child2 share one Parent subobject.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 5 - Final-Style Closed Exam
exams.description: Lecture and Comprehensive Exams - A broad tricky closed exam covering the full lecture sequence.

questions.prompt: Which chain best shows the course progression?
choices: Pointers/functions -> classes -> overloading -> inheritance -> polymorphism -> friends/templates
Templates -> pointers -> syllabus only -> no classes
questions.explanation: The lectures build from C++ review into OOP features and then advanced tools.

questions.prompt: Which concepts directly support abstraction or hiding implementation details?
questions.explanation: All three reduce visible low-level detail in different ways.

questions.prompt: Which statement about constructors and destructors is correct?
choices: Constructors initialize; destructors clean up when objects are destroyed.
Destructors can be overloaded by parameters.
questions.explanation: Constructors prepare objects; destructors run during destruction and cannot take parameters.

questions.prompt: Which value is displayed?
choices: Undefined because private members cannot be accessed inside class
questions.explanation: The member function can access private marks of both Student objects and computes average.
questions.code:
Student st1(70), st2(60);
st1.calculateAverage(st1, st2);

questions.prompt: Which overload pair is invalid if only the return type differs?
choices: int sum(int, int) and double sum(int, int)
int sum(int) and int sum(int, int)
int sum(int, double) and int sum(double, int)
double sum(double, double) and int sum(int, int)
questions.explanation: Overload resolution cannot distinguish by return type alone.

questions.prompt: Which phrase best describes polymorphism?
questions.explanation: Polymorphism lets the same call shape invoke different behavior.

questions.prompt: A friend class makes all member functions of that class friends of the granting class.
questions.explanation: Declaring a friend class grants its member functions access.

questions.prompt: Which is the strongest reason to avoid unnecessary friends?
choices: They force all data to be static.
questions.explanation: Friends are useful, but they reduce strict data hiding.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 6 - Advanced Mixed Review
exams.description: Advanced Full-Lecture Exams - Full lecture coverage with tricky closed-choice questions from review through templates.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: p starts at a[0]. ++p moves to a[1], so *p is 5.

questions.prompt: Which function parameter most clearly receives an array by pointer?
questions.explanation: int* p receives the address of the first integer. int p[] in a parameter also decays to int*, but the direct pointer form is clearest here.

questions.prompt: Which programming style is closest to writing every exact step of an algorithm?
questions.explanation: Imperative programming describes how to do the task step by step.

questions.prompt: Which items are usually bundled inside an object in OOP?
questions.explanation: Objects combine data and behavior for a meaningful entity or responsibility.

questions.prompt: Which class declaration correctly makes balance hidden but deposit usable outside?
choices: class Account { private: double balance; public: void deposit(double); };
class Account { public: double balance; private: void deposit(double); };
class Account { double balance; private: void deposit(double); };
class Account { public: private double balance; void deposit(double); };
questions.explanation: Private data plus public member functions is the usual encapsulation pattern.

questions.prompt: What is wrong with declaring a constructor as void Student()?
choices: A constructor has no return type.
A constructor must always be private.
A constructor must always return int.
questions.explanation: Constructors use the class name and do not specify a return type.

questions.prompt: A destructor name begins with ~ followed by the class name.
questions.explanation: For class Student, the destructor is written as ~Student().

questions.prompt: A static data member is useful when:
choices: All objects must share one class-wide value
Every object must have a unique copy only
The data must be invisible to member functions
questions.explanation: Static class data belongs to the class as a shared value, not to each individual object.

questions.prompt: What is printed if count is static and the constructor increments it?
questions.explanation: Three objects are constructed, so the shared static count becomes 3.

questions.prompt: Which calls can match overloaded constructors Student() and Student(int)?
questions.explanation: No-argument and int construction are supported. A string argument is not supported unless such a constructor exists.

questions.prompt: Which operator overload declaration is for a binary + member function?
questions.explanation: A member binary operator+ commonly receives the right operand as a parameter and returns a result object.

questions.prompt: In member operator overloading, what does this normally represent?
questions.explanation: For a member operator, this points to the object on the left side of the operator.

questions.prompt: Private inheritance means the base class private members become public in the derived class.
questions.explanation: Private base members are never directly accessible in the derived class, and public/protected inherited members become private under private inheritance.

questions.prompt: Which keyword enables runtime dispatch for an overridden base-class function?
questions.explanation: virtual tells C++ to resolve the call based on the actual object at runtime.

questions.prompt: Which declaration best prevents diamond inheritance from storing two Parent subobjects?
questions.explanation: virtual public Parent shares the Parent base subobject across diamond paths.

questions.prompt: A friend function is not a member, so how is it usually called?
questions.explanation: A friend function has ordinary function-call syntax; it is not called with the dot operator as a member.

questions.prompt: Which statements about templates are correct?
choices: They make runtime polymorphism unnecessary in every case.
questions.explanation: Templates are compile-time generic programming tools; they do not replace every need for runtime polymorphism.

questions.prompt: What is the correct result?
choices: The function cannot work with int
questions.explanation: The template swap receives references, so it exchanges the original variables.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 7 - Tricky Code Concepts
exams.description: Advanced Full-Lecture Exams - Full lecture coverage with emphasis on code tracing, access control, and overload traps.

questions.prompt: Which expression reads the third element of array a using pointer notation?
questions.explanation: The third element has index 2, so *(a + 2) reads it.

questions.prompt: When an array is passed to a function, the function automatically knows how many elements are in it.
questions.explanation: The function usually receives a pointer to the first element; length must be passed separately or otherwise known.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: p points to n. Assigning through *p changes n to 9.

questions.prompt: Which paradigm best matches breaking a program into named functions like input(), calculate(), display()?
questions.explanation: Procedural programming organizes work into procedures/functions.

questions.prompt: Which are signs of encapsulation?
questions.explanation: Encapsulation protects data and exposes controlled operations.

questions.prompt: Which outside definition is correct for member function void display() of class Elevator?
questions.explanation: The scope resolution operator attaches the function definition to the Elevator class.

questions.prompt: What is a default constructor?
choices: A constructor callable with no arguments
A constructor that must be virtual
questions.explanation: A default constructor can create an object without passing arguments.

questions.prompt: Which constructor is selected?
questions.explanation: Box b(4) passes one integer, so Box(int) is selected.

questions.prompt: Which overload set is valid?
questions.explanation: Different parameter types create a valid overload. Return type alone is not enough.

questions.prompt: Which statement about object arguments is true?
choices: A member function can access private data of another object of the same class.
Objects must always be global to pass them.
Private data cannot be read by any member function.
questions.explanation: Member functions of a class can access private members of any object of that same class.

questions.prompt: Which prefix ++ overload can support c2 = ++c1?
questions.explanation: The prefix operator must return a Counter value or reference if its result is assigned.

questions.prompt: Which object is the implicit left operand in total = a + b when + is a member of Item?
questions.explanation: The left operand a calls the member operator+.

questions.prompt: Which are valid inheritance types from the lecture list?
questions.explanation: Parameter inheritance is not one of the listed OOP inheritance types.

questions.prompt: In public inheritance, protected base members become:
questions.explanation: Public inheritance keeps protected members protected.

questions.prompt: A virtual function must be called through a base pointer or reference to demonstrate runtime polymorphism clearly.
questions.explanation: Virtual dispatch is most clearly observed when a base pointer/reference refers to a derived object.

questions.prompt: Why can a virtual destructor be useful?
choices: To destroy derived objects correctly through base pointers
questions.explanation: A virtual destructor ensures the derived destructor runs when deleting through a base pointer.

questions.prompt: Which statement about friend classes is true?
choices: Friendship makes both classes the same class.
questions.explanation: A class grants friendship to another; the relationship is not automatically reciprocal.

questions.prompt: Which template syntax declares two type parameters?
questions.explanation: Multiple type parameters are listed inside the template angle brackets.

questions.prompt: Which result is expected?
choices: Compilation error because templates cannot compare char
questions.explanation: For numbers, a simple getMax template returns the larger value.
questions.code:
myclass<int> obj(4, 9);
cout << obj.getMax();

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 8 - Concept Trap Exam
exams.description: Advanced Full-Lecture Exams - Tricky conceptual traps across all lectures with closed-choice answers only.

questions.prompt: Which is the most precise meaning of &x?
questions.explanation: & is the address-of operator.

questions.prompt: Which is the most precise meaning of *p when p stores a valid address?
choices: The value at the address stored in p
questions.explanation: * dereferences the pointer, reading or writing the pointed-to object.

questions.prompt: Which ideas belong to the C++ review before OOP classes?
questions.explanation: Virtual base classes come later with inheritance and polymorphism.

questions.prompt: The cake recipe example in paradigms mainly illustrates:
questions.explanation: The recipe describes exact steps, which matches imperative thinking.

questions.prompt: A multi-paradigm language can support more than one programming style.
questions.explanation: Languages like C++ can support procedural, object-oriented, and generic styles.

questions.prompt: Which class member should usually be private in a bank account class?
questions.explanation: balance is internal state and should be protected by controlled operations.

questions.prompt: Which setter best shows validation?
choices: void setAge(int a){ if(a >= 0) age = a; }
questions.explanation: The setter checks the incoming value before storing it.

questions.prompt: What is constructor overloading?
choices: Defining multiple constructors with different parameter lists
Changing only the constructor return type
questions.explanation: Constructor overloads differ by parameters, just like function overloads.

questions.prompt: Which value is printed?
choices: Compilation error because count is private
questions.explanation: inc_count is public and increments the private count twice through member access.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes class functions and object data in memory?
choices: Each object has its own data; objects share the same member function code.
Each object has a full separate copy of all function code.
Member functions exist only after a destructor runs.
questions.explanation: Objects have separate ordinary data, while member function code is shared.

questions.prompt: Why is operator overloading useful for objects?
choices: It makes object operations more readable when the meaning is natural.
questions.explanation: d3 = d1 + d2 is clearer than d3.addobjects(d1, d2) when + has a meaningful object interpretation.

questions.prompt: At least one operand of an overloaded operator must involve a user-defined type.
questions.explanation: C++ does not allow redefining operators only for built-in types.

questions.prompt: Which inheritance form is Child : public Parent?
questions.explanation: One derived class from one base class is single inheritance.

questions.prompt: Which inherited members can a derived class directly use from a public base class?
questions.explanation: Derived classes can directly access inherited public and protected members, but not private members.

questions.prompt: If print() is virtual, what is called?
questions.explanation: The base pointer refers to a Derived object, and virtual dispatch calls the derived override.
questions.code:
Base* p = new Derived;
p->print();

questions.prompt: In a diamond inheritance problem, ambiguity occurs because:
choices: There may be two inherited copies of the same base subobject.
questions.explanation: Without virtual inheritance, two paths can create two copies of the same base class.

questions.prompt: Which friend declaration allows function add to access private data from class A?
questions.explanation: friend appears before the ordinary function declaration inside the class.

questions.prompt: Which is a correct reason to use a class template?
choices: The class logic is same but the data type changes.
The class must have no member variables.
The class must be friend of every other class.
questions.explanation: Class templates avoid duplicate classes for int, double, char, and other types when behavior is the same.

questions.prompt: template<typename T> and template<class T> are generally equivalent for type parameters.
questions.explanation: For ordinary type template parameters, both forms are commonly accepted.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 9 - Output and Design Choices
exams.description: Advanced Full-Lecture Exams - Full lecture coverage with design-choice questions and code-output traps.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: p++ moves from a[0] to a[1], printing 20. Then p++ moves to a[2], printing 30.

questions.prompt: Which item is best modeled as a struct in the review lecture?
choices: A grouped student record with number and name
questions.explanation: A struct groups related fields like student number, name, and address.

questions.prompt: Which paradigm treats functions as first-class citizens most strongly?
questions.explanation: Functional programming emphasizes functions as values and pure functions.

questions.prompt: OOP was introduced in the lecture as a response to:
choices: Large programs becoming hard to manage as long procedures
The removal of functions from C++
questions.explanation: OOP separates concerns into objects when one long procedural program becomes difficult to manage.

questions.prompt: Which object creation calls a no-argument constructor?
questions.explanation: Elevator e; creates an object using the no-argument constructor.

questions.prompt: What should a destructor usually be responsible for?
choices: Cleanup when an object is destroyed
questions.explanation: A destructor is called during object destruction to release resources or perform cleanup.

questions.prompt: Which facts about static class data are correct?
choices: It is shared by all objects.
It must be defined outside the class in many pre-C++17 examples.
It is useful for object counters.
It is copied separately for every object.
questions.explanation: The lecture counter example uses one shared static value for all instances.

questions.prompt: Which is the best reason object parameters can be useful?
choices: They let one object compute using another object's data when allowed.
They remove the need for classes.
questions.explanation: Examples like Distance addition and Student average pass objects into functions for object-based calculations.

questions.prompt: What does temp.count = count; usually do in operator++?
choices: Copies the incremented value into the temporary return object
questions.explanation: A named temporary object can be filled with the new state and returned.

questions.prompt: Why does int + Object often need a non-member or friend operator overload?
choices: The left operand is not an object of the class, so no member operator is found there.
questions.explanation: Member operators are looked up from the left operand's class; int has no user-defined member operator for your class.

questions.prompt: Multiple inheritance means one class can inherit from two or more base classes.
questions.explanation: For example, Bat can inherit from Mammal and Winged_Animal.

questions.prompt: Which declaration shows multiple inheritance?
choices: class Bat : public Mammal, public Winged_Animal
class Bat : public Mammal : public Winged_Animal
questions.explanation: C++ separates multiple base classes with commas after the colon.

questions.prompt: Which access specifier allows derived classes to access a member but blocks normal outside access?
questions.explanation: protected is available to the class and derived classes, but not ordinary outside code.

questions.prompt: Which virtual function rule is correct?
choices: Virtual functions are only for templates.
questions.explanation: The lecture states virtual functions cannot be static.

questions.prompt: Which are signs of runtime polymorphism?
questions.explanation: Runtime polymorphism combines virtual functions, inheritance, and base references/pointers to derived objects.

questions.prompt: Why can friend functions be useful for operator overloading?
choices: They can access private data while allowing non-member left operands.
They force all operators to be unary.
questions.explanation: Friends can implement non-member operators that still inspect private object data.

questions.prompt: A friend function can be declared in public, private, or protected sections.
questions.explanation: The access label does not change the meaning of the friend declaration.

questions.prompt: Which call explicitly asks a template function to use double?
questions.explanation: Explicit template arguments are placed after the function name in angle brackets.

questions.prompt: What does T represent in template<class T>?
questions.explanation: T is a type parameter substituted by the compiler when the template is instantiated.

exams.title: All Lectures Exam 10 - Full Coverage Challenge
exams.description: Advanced Full-Lecture Exams - A final-style closed-choice challenge spanning all lecture topics.

questions.prompt: Which line correctly declares a pointer to int?
questions.explanation: int* p declares p as a pointer that can store the address of an int.

questions.prompt: What is printed?
questions.explanation: p points to a[1], so *(p + 1) refers to a[2], which is 3.

questions.prompt: Which data structure from the review groups student number and name before classes are introduced?
questions.explanation: Structs group related fields and are used in the review lecture before class details.

questions.prompt: Which paradigm hides loop mechanics behind functions like filter or sort?
questions.explanation: Declarative programming emphasizes the desired result and hides many implementation steps.

questions.prompt: Which are core OOP concepts mentioned across the lectures?
questions.explanation: Encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism are central OOP pillars in the lectures.

questions.prompt: Which class syntax is closest to the lecture's general pattern?
choices: class Student { private: int no; public: void set(int); };
questions.explanation: C++ class syntax uses class Name { access sections and members };

questions.prompt: A public member function can read and modify private data of its own object.
questions.explanation: Private blocks outside direct access, not access by the class's own member functions.

questions.prompt: Which member is called automatically when an object is created?
questions.explanation: Constructors initialize objects when they are created.

questions.prompt: Which member is called automatically when an object is destroyed?
questions.explanation: The destructor runs when the object's lifetime ends.

questions.prompt: Which variable should be static for counting how many objects were created?
choices: A local variable inside main only
questions.explanation: A static class counter is shared and can track all constructed objects.

questions.prompt: Which are valid reasons for overloaded constructors?
choices: Allow objects to start with different initialization inputs
questions.explanation: Constructor overloads provide different parameter lists for different initialization needs.

questions.prompt: What does operator++() without an int parameter usually represent?
questions.explanation: Prefix ++ is usually operator++(); postfix uses operator++(int).

questions.prompt: What does operator++(int) usually represent?
questions.explanation: The dummy int parameter distinguishes postfix ++.

questions.prompt: Which operator+ return creates an unnamed temporary object?
questions.explanation: Items(count) constructs a temporary object and returns it.

questions.prompt: Which relationship is best modeled by inheritance?
choices: Student has a random unrelated integer
questions.explanation: Inheritance models is-a relationships when public inheritance is appropriate.

questions.prompt: Which inheritance mode is usually used for an is-a relationship?
questions.explanation: Public inheritance preserves the base interface as part of the derived object.

questions.prompt: A virtual base class can reduce ambiguity in multiple inheritance.
questions.explanation: Virtual inheritance shares a common base subobject and prevents duplicate base ambiguity.

questions.prompt: Which access can a friend function receive?
choices: Private and protected members of the granting class
questions.explanation: Friend functions can access private and protected members of the class that declares them as friends.

questions.prompt: Which are good template use cases?
questions.explanation: Function and class templates are useful for generic algorithms and containers, but they do not automatically replace runtime polymorphism.
